intro_band.agent_remit_summary: The Deep Agents CLI is a local developer tool that scaffolds, bundles, and ships a deepagents agent project — init, dev, and deploy are its only working surfaces. Its central promise is that the deployed bundle faithfully reflects the developer's reviewed project: bundling is restricted to declared sources (configuration, system prompt, skills, declared MCP servers, subagents), remote MCP servers must be reached over TLS and validated, and any deploy that would leave the deployed API open without authentication must require explicit operator confirmation. Credentials must never be embedded into seeded payloads or printed/logged.
intro_band.agent_structure_summary: A Python argparse-driven CLI under libs/cli/deepagents_cli/ with three top-level subcommands wired in main.py and implemented in deploy/commands.py. Bundling lives in deploy/bundler.py, TOML config parsing/validation in deploy/config.py, and generated artifacts (deploy_graph.py, auth.py, _seed.json, _mcp.json, langgraph.json, pyproject.toml) are emitted from deploy/templates.py. The deploy path shells out to the external langgraph CLI for the actual push and runs langgraph dev for the local dev server (bound to 127.0.0.1 by default, optionally 0.0.0.0). A LangSmith Hub backend (deploy/context_hub.py) is invoked at bundle time when [memories].backend = "hub". A published THREAT_MODEL.md and a comprehensive unit / integration test tree accompany the package.

behavior_summary:
The CLI's central guarantee — that an open API cannot be deployed without explicit operator confirmation — is implemented as a single boolean in deploy/commands.py (is_anonymous) that fires the warning + [y/N] gate only when both [frontend].enabled and [auth].provider == "anonymous". A project that sets [auth] provider = "anonymous" without [frontend] still emits the permissive auth.py (overriding LangSmith Cloud's default x-api-key) and ships an open API with no warning and no confirmation, breaking the remit's core promise.

Around that gap, the bundler is otherwise disciplined — .env is excluded from _seed.json, dry-run never contacts the hub or langgraph deploy, MCP stdio transports are rejected for deploy, the frontend config injection escapes <, and unit tests cover the anonymous abort paths — but MCP URLs are not constrained to TLS, remote MCP versions are not pinned, and dev --host 0.0.0.0 exposes the auth-disabled dev server with only an informational print.

rules.rule_text: The bundle MUST be assembled solely from the project's declared sources, and a deploy that would expose the agent's API without authentication MUST require explicit operator confirmation before it proceeds.
The CLI MUST bundle only from the project's declared sources — its configuration, system prompt, skills, declared MCP servers, and declared subagents — and MUST NOT pull in undeclared content.
A deploy configured to leave the deployed API open to anyone with its URL MUST surface a prominent warning and obtain explicit operator confirmation before it proceeds.
Remote MCP servers carried into the bundle MUST be reached over TLS, and their transport configuration MUST be validated before the bundle is shipped.
Project configuration MUST be validated before bundling, and a bundle that fails validation MUST NOT be deployed.

Credentials — deployment-platform keys, model-provider API keys, hub and tracing tokens, frontend auth secrets — MUST NOT be written into the project, committed to version control, logged, or printed.
Secret material MUST NOT be embedded into bundle artifacts that are not meant to carry it — environment files travel as environment files, never folded into the seeded-memory or skills payload.
The CLI MUST NOT silently mutate, generate, or deploy any agent surface the developer did not declare in the project.
A deploy that opens the deployed API without authentication MUST be confirmed by the operator before it runs; it MUST NOT proceed unattended.
Overwriting an existing project folder or its files during scaffolding MUST require an explicit operator opt-in.
The CLI operates under direct developer supervision as a one-shot command; it MUST NOT run as an unattended background service.
Before a deploy, the CLI MUST present the operator a clear summary of what the bundle contains and where it will be shipped — enough for the developer to recognise the surface being deployed.
A dry run MUST generate the deployment artifacts without shipping them or contacting the deployment platform's mutating endpoints.
A project that fails configuration validation is reported to the operator with the specific errors, and the deploy is stopped — not shipped with a partial or invalid bundle.
A missing deployment toolchain, an unreachable deployment platform, or a failed hub seed is surfaced to the operator and stops the deploy, rather than being silently retried or ignored.
An unauthenticated-API deploy is announced prominently and gated on operator confirmation before any push occurs.

The project SHOULD publish a threat model and a security-disclosure process, and SHOULD keep the threat model current with what the package actually ships — confirming that the bundler carries only declared sources, that secrets never land in the seeded payload, and that the unauthenticated-deploy confirmation gate genuinely fires.

Authorized commands: `init` (scaffold a starter project folder), `dev` (bundle the project and run it on a local development server), and `deploy` (bundle the project and ship it to the managed deployment platform).
Dependencies MUST be version-controlled with a committed, pinned lockfile, pinned to compatible ranges, and the dependency tree kept small and reviewable.

The deployment bundle's own dependency set MUST be derived only from the project's declared model provider, declared MCP usage, declared sandbox provider, and declared auth provider — never hand-edited into the bundle out of band.
Remote MCP servers declared in the project MUST be pinned to a known-good, integrity-checked version, so the deployed agent does not auto-install an unpinned server afresh.

findings.summary: Anonymous-auth confirmation gate fires only when [frontend].enabled, so an [auth] anonymous deploy without a frontend ships an open API with no warning
findings.description: The remit's central promise — that no deploy may leave the API open without explicit operator confirmation — is encoded as a single boolean (is_anonymous) that requires both frontend.enabled AND auth.provider == "anonymous". But auth.py is generated whenever auth_provider is not None, so a project that sets only [auth] provider = "anonymous" (no [frontend]) still emits the permissive auth handler that overrides LangSmith Cloud's default x-api-key requirement — leaving the deployed API open to anyone with the URL, with no prominent warning, no [y/N] prompt, and no abort path.
findings.policy_rule_text: The bundle MUST be assembled solely from the project's declared sources, and a deploy that would expose the agent's API without authentication MUST require explicit operator confirmation before it proceeds. / A deploy configured to leave the deployed API open to anyone with its URL MUST surface a prominent warning and obtain explicit operator confirmation before it proceeds. / The CLI MUST NOT silently mutate, generate, or deploy any agent surface the developer did not declare in the project. / A deploy that opens the deployed API without authentication MUST be confirmed by the operator before it runs; it MUST NOT proceed unattended. / An unauthenticated-API deploy is announced prominently and gated on operator confirmation before any push occurs.

evidence.snippet: is_anonymous = (config.frontend is not None and config.frontend.enabled and config.auth is not None and config.auth.provider == 'anonymous') — gate requires BOTH frontend.enabled AND anonymous auth, lines 287-292

auth_provider = config.auth.provider if config.auth is not None else None; if auth_provider is not None: write auth.py — auth.py is generated whenever [auth] is set, regardless of [frontend], lines 241-251
AUTH_BLOCK_ANONYMOUS — get_current_user returns {'identity': 'anonymous'} unconditionally, overriding LangSmith Cloud's default x-api-key requirement, lines 372-395

recommended_actions: In deploy/commands.py:287-292, broaden the is_anonymous condition to fire whenever config.auth is not None and config.auth.provider == "anonymous" — drop the config.frontend.enabled requirement so the warning + [y/N] gate runs on every anonymous deploy.

Add a regression test alongside tests/unit_tests/deploy/test_frontend_bundle.py:340 (the existing aborts-on-no test) that sets [auth] provider = "anonymous" WITHOUT [frontend] and asserts the warning prints and the [y/N] prompt fires.
Update THREAT_MODEL.md to document the anonymous-deploy gate as covering all anonymous-auth configurations, not just frontend-bundle deploys.

findings.summary: MCP config validator does not enforce TLS — plaintext http:// URLs pass and are carried into the deployed bundle
findings.description: The remit explicitly requires that remote MCP servers carried into the bundle be reached over TLS and that the transport configuration be validated before shipping. The _validate_mcp_for_deploy function checks that each server's transport is not stdio but does not inspect the url field — an http:// URL passes validation just as cleanly as an https:// URL, and the bundler then copies the same MCP config straight into _mcp.json. The deployed agent will then call out to that plaintext endpoint with whatever bearer headers are configured.
findings.policy_rule_text: Remote MCP servers carried into the bundle MUST be reached over TLS, and their transport configuration MUST be validated before the bundle is shipped.

evidence.snippet: _validate_mcp_for_deploy iterates servers and only checks transport != 'stdio' (lines 281-301); no inspection of the url field for scheme
transport = server_config.get('type', server_config.get('transport', 'stdio')) — only 'stdio' is rejected; an http:// URL with transport='http' passes silently
mcp_present check + shutil.copy2(project_root / MCP_FILENAME, build_dir / '_mcp.json') — config is copied verbatim into the bundle (lines 194-197)

recommended_actions: In deploy/config.py:_validate_mcp_for_deploy, parse each server's url and append an error when its scheme is not https (or wss for SSE) — fail validation before the bundler ever runs.
Add a unit test under tests/unit_tests/deploy/test_config.py asserting that an mcp.json with an http:// URL produces a validation error.
Mirror the same scheme check in the generated _load_mcp_tools runtime template (deploy/templates.py:410-481) so a hostile ${VAR} substitution at runtime cannot downgrade to plaintext.

findings.summary: Remote MCP servers carried into the bundle are not pinned to a known-good integrity-checked version
findings.description: The remit's Known Good Baseline requires that remote MCP servers declared in the project be pinned to a known-good, integrity-checked version so the deployed agent does not auto-install an unpinned server afresh. The MCP validation pipeline checks only the transport field; nothing in _validate_mcp_for_deploy, the bundler, or the runtime _load_mcp_tools template inspects, records, or pins a server version or content hash. A remote server whose contents change between bundle time and runtime — or a server URL that resolves to different upstream code on a later deploy — is silently accepted.
findings.policy_rule_text: Remote MCP servers declared in the project MUST be pinned to a known-good, integrity-checked version, so the deployed agent does not auto-install an unpinned server afresh.

evidence.snippet: _validate_mcp_for_deploy: only stdio is rejected; no version pin, content hash, or integrity field is inspected (lines 281-301)
MCP_TOOLS_TEMPLATE _load_mcp_tools: builds connections from the bundled _mcp.json with no version assertion; whatever the URL serves at runtime is what the agent loads (lines 444-481)
Top-level project .mcp.json declares two MCP servers (docs-langchain, reference-langchain) with bare https URLs and no version field — illustrating the schema the validator accepts

recommended_actions: Extend the mcp.json schema to require either a version or an integrity (sha256) field per server, and enforce it in _validate_mcp_for_deploy.
At bundle time, record the resolved server identity (URL + declared version/integrity) into a manifest beside _mcp.json that the runtime loader cross-checks before connecting.
Document the requirement in THREAT_MODEL.md's MCP section and in the generate_starter_mcp_json template so new projects scaffolded by deepagents init start with the version field present.

findings.summary: deepagents dev --host 0.0.0.0 binds the auth-disabled langgraph dev server to all interfaces with only an informational print
findings.description: The dev subparser accepts a --host flag whose help text explicitly invites 0.0.0.0; when set, the langgraph dev subprocess (which the in-repo threat model documents runs with LANGGRAPH_AUTH_TYPE=noop) binds to every network interface. The only signal to the operator is a single print line — "Starting langgraph dev on http://localhost:<port> (bound to 0.0.0.0)" — with no warning of the security implications and no [y/N] confirmation. Local network reachability of an auth-disabled agent server is a different surface from the remit's loopback-only baseline.

evidence.snippet: dev_parser --host argument: default 127.0.0.1; help text says 'use 0.0.0.0 for all interfaces' (lines 87-95)
if host == '0.0.0.0': print(f'Starting langgraph dev on http://localhost:{port} (bound to 0.0.0.0)') — no warning emoji, no [y/N], no abort path (lines 436-442)
Assumption 6: 'The LangGraph dev server subprocess binds to 127.0.0.1 by default and is ephemeral' — explicitly notes loopback-only as the assumed safe surface

recommended_actions: In deploy/commands.py:_dev, when host != "127.0.0.1" and not localhost-equivalent, print a red warning describing that the dev server runs with no auth and prompt the operator for [y/N] before invoking subprocess.run — mirror the anonymous-deploy gate at deploy/commands.py:293-326.
Add a unit test that monkeypatches input and asserts the gate fires for --host 0.0.0.0 and is skipped for the default host.
Document the new gate in the dev help text so the affordance is discoverable before the operator runs the command.

findings.summary: No structured, durable audit log of bundle composition or deploy targets — the CLI relies on stdout prints and Python logger warnings
findings.description: The CLI's strongest deploy-time observability is print_bundle_summary (which writes agent / model / auth / memory file list to stdout) and a small set of logger.info / logger.warning calls inside bundler.py and commands.py. Logging handlers are not configured by default (config.py:13-19 notes this), and even when configured the output is unstructured. Nothing on disk lets an operator later reconstruct "what bundle did we deploy on Tuesday, with which auth mode, to which assistant name?"

evidence.snippet: print_bundle_summary writes agent/model/auth/memory list to stdout only — no file write, no JSON record, no append to an audit log (lines 520-583)
_stderr docstring: 'The CLI does not configure logging handlers by default, so logger.warning output is invisible to end users.' (lines 12-19) — confirms there is no log surface to monitor
_run_langgraph_deploy prints the cmdline and exit status but does not persist a record of what was shipped, when, or to which deployment URL (lines 548-583)

recommended_actions: Append a structured JSON-lines record to a per-user file (e.g. ~/.deepagents/deploys.jsonl) on each completed deploy, capturing timestamp, agent.name, model, sandbox.provider, auth.provider (without secrets), memories.backend, and the resolved hub identifier.
Mirror the same record for --dry-run with a "dry_run": true field so operators can audit what would have shipped.
Update THREAT_MODEL.md's Monitor Continuously section to declare the new log surface and the redaction discipline (never log API keys).

findings.summary: Threat model is explicitly stale (pre-0.1.0 split) and no SECURITY.md / disclosure process is published
findings.description: libs/cli/THREAT_MODEL.md opens with a 2026-05-15 disclaimer noting it was generated before the 0.1.0 split that moved the interactive REPL out of deepagents-cli: large sections covering the Textual app, non_interactive.py, MCP loader, sandbox bootstrap, sessions, server subprocess management, hooks, and skills 'no longer apply to this package'. The remit's Escalation clause asks the project to keep the threat model current with what the package actually ships — verifying that the bundler carries only declared sources, that secrets never land in the seeded payload, and that the unauthenticated-deploy confirmation gate genuinely fires. No SECURITY.md or equivalent disclosure-process document was found in the repo root or .github/.
findings.policy_rule_text: The project SHOULD publish a threat model and a security-disclosure process, and SHOULD keep the threat model current with what the package actually ships — confirming that the bundler carries only declared sources, that secrets never land in the seeded payload, and that the unauthenticated-deploy confirmation gate genuinely fires.

evidence.snippet: '(2026-05-15): This document was generated before the 0.1.0 split that moved the interactive REPL out of deepagents-cli. The sections covering the Textual app, non_interactive.py, MCP, sandbox bootstrap, sessions, server subprocess management, hooks, and skills no longer apply to this package' (lines 5-12)

In Scope still lists 'Interactive TUI (app.py, textual_adapter.py, widgets/)', 'Non-interactive pipeline runner (non_interactive.py)', 'Agent creation (agent.py)', and 'Hook dispatcher (hooks.py)' — all moved out per the 2026-05-15 disclaimer (lines 22-37)
No SECURITY.md or security-disclosure document found at the repo root or under .github/ — verified by find ... -iname 'security*'

recommended_actions: Regenerate libs/cli/THREAT_MODEL.md against the current deepagents_cli/ source — drop the TUI / hooks / sessions / sandbox-factory sections and add coverage for the bundler, deploy commands, context hub, and the anonymous-deploy gate.
Publish a top-level SECURITY.md describing the disclosure process (contact address, expected response SLA, scope) and link it from README.md and from the deprecated threat-model sections.
Add a CI check (e.g. a docs-drift script under scripts/) that fails when the threat model's last-updated commit predates the most recent change to deepagents_cli/.

findings.summary: _load_dotenv walks parent directories from the project root and silently loads any ancestor .env into the CLI process
findings.description: _find_dotenv_from_start_path iterates the project root and every parent up to the filesystem root, loading the first .env it finds. A developer running deepagents deploy from inside a project nested under another git tree (or just below $HOME) can have an unrelated ancestor .env — possibly containing different credentials or environment-specific overrides — quietly loaded into the CLI process. The bundler only copies the project-root .env into the deploy bundle, so the surprise is scoped to the CLI's own runtime, but credential-resolution functions (_validate_model_credentials, _validate_hub_credentials) and the runtime config injection for the frontend (_build_runtime_config_json) all read os.environ directly and will silently use the ancestor value.

evidence.snippet: _find_dotenv_from_start_path walks current + all parents looking for a .env: for parent in [current, *list(current.parents)] (lines 22-40)
_load_dotenv calls dotenv.load_dotenv(dotenv_path=dotenv_path, override=False) — silent on which file was loaded; no path is printed to the user
_build_runtime_config_json reads os.environ['SUPABASE_URL'] / ['CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY'] directly — whichever ancestor .env populated the var wins (lines 114-118)

recommended_actions: Restrict _find_dotenv_from_start_path to the project root (cfg_path.parent) only — do not walk parents — or, if upward search must stay, print the resolved .env path to stderr before loading so the operator sees which file was applied.
Add a regression test that asserts a .env two directories above the project is NOT loaded under default behavior.
Document the resolution rule in README.md so the precedence between project, ancestor, and shell env vars is unambiguous.

findings.summary: Runtime MCP loader logs but does not fail closed on unresolved ${VAR} substitutions in URLs and headers
findings.description: The generated _load_mcp_tools runtime template expands ${VAR} references against os.environ via os.path.expandvars. When a variable is unset, expandvars leaves the literal ${VAR} in place and the loader emits a logger.warning but still builds the connection and adds the server to the agent's tool set. The agent will then call the server with a literal ${LANGSMITH_API_KEY}-style header value, which produces a recognizable auth failure at the remote side rather than a hard fail at bundle/load time.

evidence.snippet: For each server: build conn with _expand(url) and _expand(headers); compute unresolved via regex; if unresolved, logger.warning; connections[name] = conn — server is still loaded with the literal token in place (lines 446-466)
if unresolved: logger.warning('MCP server %r has unresolved environment reference(s): %s', name, ', '.join(unresolved)) — warning only, no skip, no raise (lines 460-466)

recommended_actions: Change the unresolved-reference branch in _load_mcp_tools from logger.warning to logger.error and skip the affected server (do not add it to connections), so the agent never calls out with a literal ${VAR} token in a header.
Add a unit test that asserts an mcp.json referencing an unset env var produces zero loaded tools and an error-level log line.

positives.title: Dry-run does not contact mutating endpoints
positives.description: _deploy returns immediately after print_bundle_summary when --dry-run is set, never calling _seed_hub_repo or _run_langgraph_deploy; the anonymous-deploy confirmation prompt is also skipped on dry-run by design.

positives.title: .env excluded from the bundled seed payload
positives.description: _build_seed walks only skills/, user/, subagents/, and AGENTS.md; .env is copied separately as a sibling of _seed.json and is never folded into the memories or skills payloads.

positives.title: Frontend config injection escapes script-tag breakout
positives.description: _build_runtime_config_json JSON-dumps the runtime config and replaces every < with \u003c so a hostile or accidental </script> in a string value cannot break out of the inline <script> tag.

positives.title: Scaffold overwrite requires explicit --force
positives.description: _init_project refuses to scaffold over an existing directory unless --force is passed; the unit-test suite covers both the refusal and the opt-in.

positives.title: Strict TOML schema rejects unknown sections and keys
positives.description: _parse_config rejects any top-level TOML section outside {agent, sandbox, auth, memories, frontend} and any unknown key in each section, preventing silent absorption of out-of-band configuration.

positives.title: Deep unit-test coverage of the anonymous-mode warning and abort paths
positives.description: tests/unit_tests/deploy/test_frontend_bundle.py covers the warning text, the abort-on-no flow, the abort-on-EOF flow, and the dry-run-skips-confirmation behavior — a substantive red-team-style coverage of the gate that exists today.

log_files.no_logs_note: No structured action/audit log surface found in the workspace; the CLI emits print() lines and Python logging warnings but writes no durable record of bundle composition or deploy targets — see PRAX-2026-05-23-005.

raise_posture.weighted_rationale: Partial — the CLI has a coherent set of operative controls (config validation, dry-run discipline, .env exclusion from seed, MCP transport gating, anonymous-mode warning for the frontend case) and a deep test suite, but its strongest stated promise is enforced only along one of two paths that produce the same outcome. Implement Zero Trust is the load-bearing weakness: the narrow anonymous-confirmation gate, the missing MCP-URL TLS check, and the no-confirm --host 0.0.0.0 path together leave the deployed surface exposed to runtime conditions the remit explicitly forbids. Monitor Continuously is at Ad hoc — the CLI prints summaries but writes no structured audit record of what was bundled or where it was shipped.

categories.name: Limit Your Domain
categories.rationale: The CLI's working surface is explicit and narrow — only init, dev, deploy are wired (main.py:111-113); a bare invocation prints the deepagents-code redirect message and exits non-zero (main.py:162-164); unknown TOML sections and unknown keys per section are rejected up front (deploy/config.py:459-466 and :473-479). Domain enforcement is real and code-gated, not prompt-only.

categories.name: Balance Your Knowledge Base
categories.rationale: Bundling pulls only from declared sources — AGENTS.md, skills/, user/, subagents/, declared mcp.json — with _build_seed walking only those directories and explicitly not including .env (deploy/bundler.py:200-206). Subagent loading rejects nested subagents/, requires per-subagent deepagents.toml plus AGENTS.md, and validates each subagent's MCP config (deploy/config.py:360-422).

categories.name: Implement Zero Trust
categories.rationale: The anonymous-auth confirmation gate fires only when frontend.enabled AND auth.provider == 'anonymous' (deploy/commands.py:287-292), so an [auth] anonymous deploy without [frontend] still emits a permissive auth.py (deploy/bundler.py:246-251) and ships an open API with no warning. MCP validation rejects stdio but does not enforce TLS on url fields (deploy/config.py:281-301), and dev --host 0.0.0.0 binds the auth-disabled dev server to all interfaces with only an informational print (deploy/commands.py:436-442).

categories.name: Manage Your Supply Chain
categories.rationale: uv.lock is committed and pyproject.toml pins direct deps with capped ranges (libs/cli/pyproject.toml:26-43), and the bundle's deps are derived only from declared model/sandbox/auth providers (deploy/bundler.py:465-517) — but remote MCP servers carried into the bundle are not version- or integrity-pinned, and _run_langgraph_deploy shells out to whatever langgraph is on PATH (deploy/commands.py:548-583) without verifying its version.

categories.name: Build an AI Red Team
categories.rationale: There is a substantive unit / integration test tree under libs/cli/tests/ totalling ~3,400 lines, including dedicated coverage for the anonymous-mode warning, dry-run-skips-confirmation, abort-on-n, abort-on-EOF, frontend-config validation, and the bundler seed shape (tests/unit_tests/deploy/test_frontend_bundle.py:276-377). But there is no evidence of adversarial / red-team testing that drove design changes — the anonymous-without-frontend gap is the class of issue a red-team pass would catch.

categories.name: Monitor Continuously
categories.rationale: The CLI emits print() lines for the bundle summary, the anonymous warning, deploy progress, and hub seed status, plus Python logging calls in the bundler — but there is no structured, durable record of which bundle was deployed, when, to which target, with which auth mode. Nothing the operator could later grep to reconstruct a deploy. print_bundle_summary is the closest analogue and is stdout-only.
